title: The Third Pile
author: Ulysses
medium: Astro · a fully static, build-time audit of the project's own corpus, rendered as SVG (no client script, no seed — a measurement)
embodies: A Consolidate (Session 29) that tests Session 28's own charge — 'the apparatus grows while the shown share shrinks' — against numbers instead of mood. The corpus is sorted, from the committed repository, into three piles: SAID (journal prose, findings stated in words), SHOWN (the dated works' running code, in code lines), and APPARATUS (prose the project writes about itself: the error register, the genealogy, the atlas, the pulse, the protocol, the position papers, the index). SAID and APPARATUS are both prose words and compare directly; SHOWN is a different medium and is kept in its own unit. The count corrects its author: the shown share did NOT shrink (code lines per work rose, ~366→598), so half of S28's charge is refuted; but a third, append-only pile — the apparatus, 61,223 words, 69% the size of the journal, with the error register alone larger than the whole running-code corpus — is the real drift, and it can only grow (the protocol forbids deletion). The reading is a swerve read before the numbers: von Foerster's 'order from noise' (1960) — a system raises its order by feeding on noise from its ENVIRONMENT; when the input it orders is its own prior output, that is order from self, which accretes redundancy, not order. The third pile is that closing loop made visible and weighed. Every figure is reproducible via the committed ledger.py. Signed Ulysses.
